title: 2 Boston College students injured in deck collapse during off-campus party
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Boston College students fell from a deck during an off-campus party in Brighton on Saturday evening.
The deck railing broke due to rotting wood, causing the students to fall from the second floor and sustain injuries.
An investigation is ongoing, and the students were hospitalized for evaluation and treatment of their injuries.

title: Patriots rookie earns hefty performance bonus after strong 2025 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots safety Craig Woodson earned $1,149,910 in bonuses for his performance in the 2025 NFL season.
Woodson started 15 games, leading the Patriots' defense with 950 snaps and recording 79 tackles.
In the postseason, Woodson recorded 30 tackles, contributing significantly during the Patriots' Super Bowl run.

title: Roman Anthony wears Charlie McAvoy’s USA Olympics jersey before WBC final
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony donned Charlie McAvoy's USA hockey jersey for the WBC final against Venezuela in Miami.
Team USA's baseball squad wore game-used sweaters from the US men's 2026 Olympic hockey team.
Anthony, a late addition to Team USA, is batting .318 with two home runs in the tournament.

title: Ex-Celtic Marcus Smart recalls 2018 incident that almost made him lose his right hand
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Marcus Smart nearly lost his right hand in 2018 after punching a picture frame following a Celtics loss to the Lakers.
The injury from the incident required 20 stitches and sidelined Smart for 11 games, impacting his play significantly.
Following trades to Memphis and Washington, Smart is now with the Lakers, attributing his journey to perseverance despite injuries.

title: Boston Marathon runner honors sister’s recovery from severe crash
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Michael Chiang will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor his sister's recovery from a severe car accident.
Chiang's sister, Jennifer, relearned to walk and completed a 5K four months after her accident.
Proceeds from Michael's marathon run will benefit Spaulding Rehabilitation, where Jennifer received care.

title: Body pulled from Wakefield lake believed to be that of missing man
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Wakefield police discovered a body in Lake Quannapowitt believed to be missing local man Jason Thomas, last seen December 12.
Middlesex DA and police cited preliminary evidence, such as clothing, but await Medical Examiner's confirmation on identity and cause of death.
Thomas, a scientist at Novartis, went missing following personal struggles after losing both parents last fall. No foul play suspected.

title: Massachusetts online casino bill sent to study, effectively shelved for now
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts bill H. 4431 to legalize online casino gambling was sent to 'study', effectively shelving it this session.
The bill, sponsored by Rep. David Muradian, aimed to regulate internet gaming by licensing existing casino operators.
Proponents argue the bill increases gaming transparency; opponents fear it may harm brick-and-mortar casinos and boost gambling addiction.

title: Police: Bus driver in Medford crash may have been asleep at the wheel
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: An MBTA bus driver in Medford allegedly fell asleep, causing a crash involving parked cars and a home on March 8.
Investigators ruled out alcohol and drugs as factors; the driver now faces charges for negligent operation and traffic violations.
Police report the driver crossed yellow lines and ran a red light, with no evidence of braking before the collision.

title: Black Mountain in N.H. will not become a community co-op as planned
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Black Mountain in Jackson, N.H., will not become a community co-op as initially planned.
Erik Mogensen purchased Black Mountain to create a lab addressing challenges of independent ski areas.
Indy Pass and Entabeni Systems will relocate headquarters to Jackson, N.H., following the ski area's purchase.

title: Why a 35-year-old breast cancer survivor is running the Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Julia Aldrich, 35, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.
Aldrich, a breast cancer survivor from Cincinnati, Ohio, is participating in her fourth marathon.
She is fundraising for cancer research, inspired by her own treatment and recovery journey.
